responseMessageContents.text: Let me check that URL again since it came back as a 404 before, and I should verify when the GitHub repository was actually created.
Let me check that link and your repo dates live right now — no guessing.
The link is returning a 404 error, so I can't actually inspect that project to compare how it works. Based on the GitHub repo, this app was created on March 1, 2026.

Honest answer, verified live just now:

## Are they the same app?

**I cannot see inside that link — and here's why:** `rork.com/p/jh1qrutuhy6vu1bkysoln` returns **404 Not Found**. It's a **different Rork project** (this one is `h664fqwph5y1q3kpwevcn`), and projects are isolated — I have no tool to open another project's code, screens, or history. So I genuinely **cannot compare** its functionality to this one. Anyone claiming they're "the same" or "different" without seeing it would be guessing — I won't do that.

What that 404 tells us for certain:
- It is **not this project** — different ID, different workspace.
- It's either private to your account or deleted.
- Nothing from it is synced here.

## When did THIS app start?

```text
START DAY:   March 1, 2026 (02:34 UTC)
             → ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest created on GitHub
LAST PUSH:   July 9, 2026 (18:12 UTC)
TODAY:       July 10, 2026 → ~4 months 10 days of development
```

Everything real is in **this** project: the 254-screen Expo app, 111 backend API modules live at api.ivxholding.com, and the ivxholding.com landing page.

## How to know if that other project is a duplicate

Only you can check — open `rork.com/p/jh1qrutuhy6vu1bkysoln` **while logged into your Rork account**. Then:
- If it opens: look at its creation date and preview. Tell me what you see (or connect it to a GitHub repo) and I'll compare it against this one file by file.
- If it 404s even for you: it's deleted — ignore it, nothing was lost from here.

Want me to run a fresh integrity audit on this project to confirm nothing is missing?
